# Spatiotemporal Critique

> A free, open-source Claude Code skill for balanced AI code and writing
> review: a preserve-first review protocol plus a multi-model critic panel —
> a genuinely independent second opinion from a different model family, so
> the review isn't the same model critiquing itself.

Replaces the one-sided "critic persona" (which invents problems, misses
intent, and breaks working parts). Runs an awake (converge) / asleep
(diverge) loop, sizes itself to the task, and can add an external-model
critic: local Ollama (free, private), subscription CLIs (OpenAI Codex),
or cloud APIs (Google Gemini, GLM, DeepSeek, Mistral, Cloudflare Workers
AI, Perplexity) — every paid call is spend-gated. Python stdlib only.

## Install
- As a Claude Code skill: `git clone https://github.com/matteolopreti/spatiotemporal-critique ~/.claude/skills/spatiotemporal-critique` — restart Claude Code, then ask for a review.
- External critic panel, zero-config: `python3 external_critic.py --init` (detects and scores local models + subscription CLIs, remembers the best panel).
- Run a review with the panel: `python3 external_critic.py yourfile --panel`

## Docs
- [README](https://github.com/matteolopreti/spatiotemporal-critique/blob/main/README.md): full protocol spec, install, FAQ, changelog
- [SKILL.md](https://github.com/matteolopreti/spatiotemporal-critique/blob/main/SKILL.md): the Claude Code skill operating procedure (paste presets)
- [EXTERNAL_CRITIC.md](https://github.com/matteolopreti/spatiotemporal-critique/blob/main/EXTERNAL_CRITIC.md): external-model setup, capability probe, API keys, cloud routing
